title: Avalanche Cross Chain - Open App
meta_description: avalanche cross chain routing for Core Bridge: connect wallet, review supported routes, and open the official Avalanche app securely.
hero_h2: Move assets across networks through Core
hero_lead: avalanche cross chain access belongs in Core, where users can connect a wallet, review the route, and open the official bridge experience for supported network transfers.
what_is: Core Bridge is the app-first route for moving supported assets between external networks and the Avalanche ecosystem. Avalanche is a multi-chain platform with a Primary Network of X-Chain, C-Chain, and P-Chain, described in the Avalanche Primary Network docs.

how.d: Go to the verified Core Bridge URL and connect a compatible wallet.
Select the source network, destination network, and supported asset shown in the app.
Check the domain, token, receiving address, route, and any wallet prompts before signing.
Approve only the transactions you understand, then track the transfer from the app or explorer.

points.p: Avalanche supports a multi-chain architecture, and its docs explain how ICM and ICTT enable cross-network messaging and token transfer patterns.
The landing page should send users directly to Core Bridge instead of imitating wallet actions or asking for seed phrases.
Users should confirm core.app, wallet prompts, token contracts, and network names before approving any bridge transaction.

faq.a: It refers to moving assets or messages across Avalanche-connected networks, including routes that involve Avalanche C-Chain, Avalanche L1s, or external chains through supported bridge infrastructure.

faq.q: Where do I open avalanche cross chain app?
faq.a: Use the official Core Bridge app at core.app/bridge and verify the domain before connecting a wallet.

faq.q: Does avalanche cross chain use the C-Chain?
faq.a: Many user-facing Avalanche DeFi routes involve the C-Chain because it is EVM-compatible, but the exact source and destination networks depend on the route shown inside Core.

faq.q: Is avalanche cross chain a separate wallet?
faq.a: No. It is an intent around cross-network movement; Core is the wallet and app surface used to access supported bridge routes.

faq.q: What should I check before an avalanche cross chain transfer?
faq.a: Verify the URL, selected networks, asset, receiving address, gas asset, and wallet approval text before signing any transaction.

footer_note: This site is not the official app; verify core.app before connecting; educational content only, not financial advice.
